message:
Implement shared library runner using dlopen/dlsym

Complete .so support with runtime loading and execution.

Changes:
- src/codegen.zig: Generate 'pub export fn pyx_main() callconv(.c) c_int' for .so
- src/main.zig: Add runSharedLib() using dlopen/dlsym
- Pass is_shared_lib flag through compilation pipeline

How it works:
1. For .so: Generate pyx_main() exported function (C calling convention)
2. For binary: Generate regular main() function (Zig convention)
3. Runner uses dlopen() to load .so at runtime
4. Calls pyx_main() via dlsym() function pointer
5. Returns 0 for success (C convention)

Usage:
  pyx myapp.py                    # Compiles myapp_arm64.so and runs it
  pyx myapp.py                    # Cached\! Instant run via dlopen
  pyx --binary myapp.py           # Compile & run standalone binary
  pyx build myapp.py              # Just build .so
  pyx build --binary myapp.py     # Just build binary

Results:
✅ Default mode now FULLY FUNCTIONAL
✅ .so files execute via dlopen/dlsym
✅ Instant cache hits (no recompilation)
✅ Both .so and binary modes work perfectly
✅ Force recompile with -f flag works

Technical details:
- dlopen() with LAZY binding (deferred symbol resolution)
- dlsym() to find pyx_main function
- C calling convention for cross-library compatibility
- Null-terminated path strings for C APIs
- Proper error handling with dlerror()

File sizes: 1.1M for both .so and binary (same runtime, different entry points)
